methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Phasecraft is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Montanaro, Cubitt, and Morton, having raised $25M to date. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage advancement. This produces an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the quantum computing space: while 7 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) yielded the minimum 3 datapoints required for robust median calculation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median point of $50M. This range reflects reasonable sensitivity around stage-progression methodology applied to the $25M raised proxy. Key uncertainties include the availability of better-resolved peer data at comparable stages and the appropriate step-up multiplier for this specific subsector; quantum computing remains early-stage with limited standardized exit multiples. The single-method reliance constrains confidence and suggests re-evaluation once additional same-stage private transactions or peer metrics become available.
